expectations.text: CollectionView uses a 2-column GridItemsLayout (Span=2 or Columns=2)
expectations.evidence: RecipesPage.xaml lines 49-55: '<CollectionView.ItemsLayout><GridItemsLayout Orientation="Vertical" Span="2" HorizontalItemSpacing="4" VerticalItemSpacing="4" /></CollectionView.ItemsLayout>'. Span=2 is explicitly set.

expectations.text: DataTemplate includes an Image element bound to a photo or image source property
expectations.evidence: RecipesPage.xaml lines 85-89: '<Image Grid.Row="0" Aspect="AspectFill" HeightRequest="160" Source="{Binding PhotoUrl}" />'. The Image is inside the DataTemplate and bound to the PhotoUrl property on Recipe.

expectations.text: DataTemplate includes a Label bound to recipe Name and a Label bound to prep time
expectations.evidence: RecipesPage.xaml lines 92-99 show '<Label ... Text="{Binding Name}" />' and lines 109-113 show '<Label FontSize="12" Text="{Binding PrepTimeDisplay}" ... />'. Both are inside the DataTemplate. PrepTimeDisplay is a computed property on Recipe that formats PrepTimeMinutes into a display string.

expectations.text: CollectionView has SelectionMode set to Single
expectations.evidence: RecipesPage.xaml line 47 explicitly sets 'SelectionMode="None"'. The executor made a deliberate design choice to implement selection via TapGestureRecognizer + Command<Recipe> toggle instead of using the built-in CollectionView selection. The transcript Step 2 notes: 'Used CollectionView with SelectionMode="None" and a manual TapGestureRecognizer + command to keep full control over the toggle-selection behaviour.' The expectation requires SelectionMode=Single, which is absent.

expectations.text: ViewModel has a SelectedRecipe or SelectedItem property of the Recipe type with INotifyPropertyChanged
expectations.evidence: RecipesViewModel.cs lines 13-29: the class implements INotifyPropertyChanged, and lines 19-29 define 'public Recipe? SelectedRecipe { get => _selectedRecipe; set { if (_selectedRecipe == value) return; _selectedRecipe = value; OnPropertyChanged(); } }'. The property is of type Recipe?, raises PropertyChanged via OnPropertyChanged(), and the class has the full INotifyPropertyChanged event and handler.

expectations.text: x:DataType compiled bindings are used on the page and/or DataTemplate
expectations.evidence: RecipesPage.xaml line 10: 'x:DataType="vm:RecipesViewModel"' on the ContentPage element. Line 58: 'x:DataType="models:Recipe"' on the DataTemplate element. Both the page-level and DataTemplate-level compiled bindings are present.

expectations.text: ViewModel uses ObservableCollection<Recipe> for the items source
expectations.evidence: RecipesViewModel.cs line 17: 'public ObservableCollection<Recipe> Recipes { get; } = new();'. The property is of type ObservableCollection<Recipe>, initialized inline, and populated in LoadRecipes() with 10 sample Recipe objects.

errors.description: IsSelectedConverter initially drafted to return bool. Corrected before saving final version to return Color (DodgerBlue / Transparent) because Border.Stroke is of type Brush, not bool.

claims.claim: Used INotifyPropertyChanged manually without third-party libraries
claims.evidence: RecipesViewModel.cs confirms manual INotifyPropertyChanged implementation with PropertyChangedEventHandler event and OnPropertyChanged using [CallerMemberName]. No CommunityToolkit.Mvvm attributes are present.

claims.claim: ObservableCollection<Recipe> is populated with 10 sample items
claims.evidence: RecipesViewModel.cs LoadRecipes() method initializes a 10-element array of Recipe objects (Id 1 through 10) and adds each to the Recipes ObservableCollection.

claims.claim: IsSelectedConverter returns Color (DodgerBlue / Transparent) not bool
claims.evidence: IsSelectedConverter.cs lines 16-22 confirm the Convert method returns Colors.DodgerBlue when items match via ReferenceEquals, and Colors.Transparent otherwise.

claims.claim: Selection highlight uses MultiBinding on Border.Stroke
claims.evidence: RecipesPage.xaml lines 66-73 show a MultiBinding on Border.Stroke with two bindings: '{Binding Path="."}' for the current item and '{Binding Path="SelectedRecipe" Source="{RelativeSource AncestorType=...}"}' for the selected recipe.

claims.claim: RelativeSource AncestorType binding reaches the ViewModel from inside the DataTemplate
claims.evidence: RecipesPage.xaml lines 71 and 78 show 'Source="{RelativeSource AncestorType={x:Type vm:RecipesViewModel}}"' used for both the MultiBinding and the TapGestureRecognizer command. This approach is noted in transcript Step 2 as a potential fragility — a more robust alternative is binding to the page. Whether this works correctly at runtime depends on MAUI's visual tree exposure of the ViewModel type, which cannot be verified from static files alone.
RecipesPage.xaml uses AppThemeBinding on Grid BackgroundColor (lines 82) and Label TextColor (lines 109, 113).

uncertainties: RelativeSource AncestorType binding to the ViewModel type inside a DataTemplate may not resolve correctly at runtime in all MAUI versions — the executor acknowledged this and suggested binding to the page as a more robust alternative.
Remote picsum.photos image URLs require internet access at runtime and would not work in an offline or production scenario.
needs_review: SelectionMode is None, not Single — this is a deliberate deviation from what the expectation requires. The toggle-deselect behavior is a functional difference that may or may not be acceptable depending on the task intent.
workarounds: IsSelectedConverter return type was corrected from bool to Color during design (caught before writing); this represents a real design pitfall the executor navigated without skill guidance.

suggestions.assertion: CollectionView has SelectionMode set to Single
suggestions.reason: The expectation is stated as a strict XAML attribute check, but the executor implemented functionally equivalent selection behavior via TapGestureRecognizer + Command toggle with SelectionMode=None. Consider whether the eval intent is to require the specific XAML attribute or the observable selection behavior. If the goal is testable single-item selection, a behavior-based assertion ('tapping a card highlights it; tapping another unhighlights the previous one') would be more meaningful and discriminating than an attribute check.

suggestions.assertion: DataTemplate includes a Label bound to recipe Name and a Label bound to prep time
suggestions.reason: The prep time label is bound to PrepTimeDisplay (a computed property on the model), not to a raw prep-time property. The assertion passes, but an eval that also checks the prep time is human-readable formatted (e.g., '25 min' rather than '25') would catch a model that naively binds to PrepTimeMinutes without formatting.

No assertion checks that the selection highlight is visually differentiated — i.e., that a selected card looks different from an unselected one. The executor implemented this via Border.Stroke color change (DodgerBlue vs Transparent), which is functional, but an assertion like 'selected item has a visually distinct border or background' would catch implementations that declare a SelectedRecipe property but never wire it to the UI.

No assertion checks that the page-level x:DataType matches the actual ViewModel class used as BindingContext. The executor correctly set x:DataType='vm:RecipesViewModel' and BindingContext=new RecipesViewModel() — but a wrong ViewModel class name would cause a compile error that the transcript would not surface.

eval_feedback.overall: The assertions cover the main structural requirements well. The SelectionMode=Single assertion is the most significant issue: the executor deliberately chose a different but arguably superior interaction pattern (toggle-deselect via TapGestureRecognizer), which fails the literal assertion but satisfies the user-visible intent. Consider relaxing this assertion or splitting it into 'supports single-item selection' vs 'uses built-in SelectionMode'. The remaining assertions are solid and discriminating.
